data.title: Primo Brands Maintained at Buy as Integration Improves and Growth Normalizes; $29 Price Target Reaffirmed
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Derek Lessard has maintained their bullish stance on PRMB stock, giving a Buy rating on April 13.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Derek Lessard has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including evidence that Primo Brands is moving past its toughest integration issues while underlying customer demand is gradually improving. He sees direct delivery operations steadily recovering, with service metrics such as on-time fulfillment and call volumes normalizing, and expects this segment to return to modest growth in the second half of the year.

At the same time, retail performance appears robust, supported by healthy scanner data, market share gains, premium product growth, and incremental distribution, which together underpin modest sales growth in the near term. Looking further out, he believes the company can resume its typical growth profile by 2027, delivering mid-single-digit revenue expansion, over $1 billion in annual free cash flow, and demonstrating resilience to higher freight and oil costs, which supports maintaining his $29 price target.

Lessard covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as MTY Food Group, Boyd Group Services, and Dorel Class B. According to TipRanks, Lessard has an average return of -1.3% and a 47.71% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 13, Barclays also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $24.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy Rating Amid Operating Turnaround and Strategic Shift Despite Lowered Price Target
data.description:
, an analyst from Intermonte, maintained the Buy rating on Energy SpA. The associated price target is €0.88.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

analyst has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the company’s operating turnaround and strategic repositioning. Energy has moved back to positive EBITDA and sharply reduced losses, signalling that the worst phase of its difficulties is likely past, even though the residential market remains under pressure.

At the same time, management is successfully shifting the business mix toward larger-scale and commercial & industrial battery projects, which typically offer stronger margins and better long‑term visibility. Despite trimming estimates and a lower target price to reflect a slower recovery and regulatory uncertainty, the analyst’s DCF valuation still indicates meaningful upside from current levels, supporting the Buy stance.

data.title: Public Storage: Hold Rating Reiterated as Solid Q1 Meets Cautious Outlook; $305 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Juan C. Sanabria maintained a Hold rating on Public Storage today and set a price target of $305.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both near-term strength and tempered forward expectations. Public Storage delivered a better-than-anticipated first quarter, with same-store NOI benefitting from an earlier tax refund and solid move-in trends as discounting eased, which should support a constructive near-term share reaction.

However, he notes that in-place rents are still weakening, and management chose to leave full-year guidance unchanged, signaling caution around the key upcoming leasing season and broader macro uncertainty. Core FFO and same-store revenue guidance remain below both Street and BMO estimates, and management did not revise ancillary or non-same-store growth expectations, leading Sanabria to reaffirm a Hold rating and keep his $305 price target unchanged.

In another report released on April 13, Evercore ISI also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $309.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Lifts Darling Ingredients Price Target to $72, Reaffirming Buy on Structural Edge in Renewable Diesel
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Jason Gabelman maintained a Buy rating on Darling Ingredients today and set a price target of $72.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jason Gabelman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to Darling Ingredients’ positioning in the renewable diesel market and favorable regulatory dynamics. He highlights that the finalized 2026–2027 Renewable Volume Obligation should keep Renewable Identification Number prices high enough to utilize all U.S. biomass-based diesel capacity and attract foreign feedstocks, effectively creating about a $1 per gallon structural edge for the DGD joint venture and supporting higher earnings power.

He projects first-quarter 2026 EBITDA roughly in line with consensus and second-quarter results only modestly softer, while still expecting core profitability to improve as feedstock pricing and DGD margins trend toward $1 per gallon over the year. On this improved outlook and anticipated $0.90 per gallon DGD EBITDA from late 2026 into 2027, he raises his estimates and lifts his price target to $72, reinforcing Darling Ingredients as a top pick in his coverage universe.

In another report released on April 22, Scotiabank also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $70.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
